meta.description: OpenAI-compatible LLM policy router. Data-plane 'router' (serve.py/shim.py) behind an 'ingress' auth-proxy (auth_proxy.py) that also serves the operator dashboard. Sigma_pol/Sigma_flow engine vendored at core/ (unhardcoded-engine).
meta.purpose: Exhaustive catalogue of user flows in entry order, to be lowered into Gherkin .feature files.
meta.architecture_note: Two HTTP layers. Consumers talk to the INGRESS (:8080): it does Bearer caller-auth, route allow-lists (403 caller_route_not_allowed), rate limits (429), usage recording, then proxies to the SHIM (router:18080) which runs the policy and owns /v1/* and operator /x/* (proxy 404s /x/* for consumers). Provider keys live in the host env; callers only ever send their own caller token.

test_flag_legend.real_money: executing for real spends on-chain funds / provider credits
test_flag_legend.real_credentials: touches a real provider credential or private key
test_flag_legend.mockable: can be exercised hermetically against the local router with set_mock_response / a stub backend
test_flag_legend.priority: P0 critical happy path, P1 important, P2 edge/advanced

flows.name: Clone the repo recursively (vendored Sigma_pol core submodule)

steps.expected: repo + core/ populated; core/router.lua and core/llm_policy.lua exist

flows.notes: Without --recursive, core/ is empty and the Docker build fails at Dockerfile:19 (test -f core/router.lua && test -f core/llm_policy.lua) -- surfaces the missing submodule early.

flows.name: Populate the core submodule after a plain clone
preconditions: did a plain git clone (no --recursive); core/ empty

steps.expected: core/ checked out at the pinned commit; core/router.lua + core/llm_policy.lua present

flows.notes: Documented in README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md.

flows.name: Create .env.secrets from .env.example
preconditions: .env, .env.secrets, secrets/ are gitignored -- never commit them

steps.expected: template created, owner-only

steps.action: fill provider keys + dashboard auth values
steps.expected: ready for router+ingress containers (both env_file .env.secrets)

flows.notes: Env vars: OPENAI_API_KEY(opt), OPENROUTER_API_KEY, CODEX_AUTH_PATH, CALLER_KEYS_JSON, CALLER_KEYS_SHA256_JSON, DASHBOARD_PASSWORD_SHA256, DASHBOARD_SESSION_SECRET, DASHBOARD_TRUSTED_USER_HEADER/SECRET, DASHBOARD_NO_AUTH, LLM_ROUTER_HOST_PORT, PUBLIC_BASE_URL, RATE_PER_MIN, BURST, LOG_LEVEL.

flows.name: Generate DASHBOARD_PASSWORD_SHA256 and DASHBOARD_SESSION_SECRET

steps.expected: 64-hex digest
urlsafe secret set

flows.notes: _dashboard_password_ok compares sha256(submitted) via hmac.compare_digest. If SESSION_SECRET unset, no session can be minted -> only NO_AUTH or trusted-header SSO work.

flows.name: Run the data-plane shim directly via nix-shell (dev, no Docker, no auth/dashboard)
flows.entry_point: nix-shell -p 'python3.withPackages(ps: with ps;[lupa httpx fastapi uvicorn pydantic])' --run 'python serve.py --config config.live.lua --default-profile default --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8080'
preconditions: submodule populated; Nix installed; provider keys exported in the shell env

steps.action: enter nix-shell with runtime deps (or shell.nix)
steps.expected: lupa/httpx/fastapi/uvicorn/pydantic available
loads env_secrets, optional model_meta refresh, inits LLMRouterHost, uvicorn serves the RAW shim (no auth_proxy, no dashboard)

flows.notes: serve.py = data-plane only. The bearer contract + dashboard exist ONLY in auth_proxy/ingress. Flags: --metrics, --default-max-tokens (0=strict), --timeout-s, --codex-auth. MODEL_META_REFRESH=0 skips the boot refresh.

flows.name: Start the default 2-service stack (router + ingress)
preconditions: .env.secrets filled; submodule populated; external docker network 'genlayer-web' exists

steps.expected: builds unhardcoded:local; router (serve.py :18080) + ingress (auth_proxy :8080). ingress depends_on router service_healthy
only loopback exposed; router never published

flows.notes: ingress env: ROUTER_UPSTREAM=http://router:18080, DASHBOARD_KEY_ENV_PATH, DASHBOARD_ISSUED_KEYS_PATH. .env.secrets bind-mounted into ingress at /run/llm-router/.env.secrets. genlayer-web is external:true -> must pre-exist.

flows.name: Start the stack with the AntSeed buyer sidecar (--profile antseed)
preconditions: default stack ok; AntSeed participation desired; a funded identity

steps.expected: also starts antseed (browse mode); writes /market/market.json + status-antseed.json into the antseed-market volume

steps.action: compose exec antseed antseed buyer status; ... network browse --services --top 5
steps.expected: sidecar up; sellers discovered

flows.notes: antseed gated behind the compose profile (omitted from default up). Wallet self-service needs ANTSEED_CONTROL_TOKEN on both router+sidecar. Needs a FUNDED wallet to transact (real money).

flows.name: Health check + smoke test the running stack

steps.expected: ingress proxies to router /healthz -> {ok:true,initialized:true}
200 model list
200; x_router shows chosen provider/model
401 Unauthorized

flows.notes: Both services define /healthz healthchecks; ingress depends_on router service_healthy.

flows.name: Local-dev: bypass dashboard auth with DASHBOARD_NO_AUTH
preconditions: local-only deployment nobody else can reach

steps.expected: every dashboard request treated as local admin
loads as admin without login; _require_dashboard_context returns {role:admin,user:'local-dev'}

flows.notes: DANGER: bypasses ALL dashboard auth. Checked FIRST, before trusted-header and session cookie. Never on a reachable deployment.

flows.name: Admin login to the dashboard with the password

steps.expected: sha256(password) compared to hash; on ok sets httponly signed cookie router_dashboard_session (role=admin,user='admin'), records login
admin role -> full access

flows.notes: Cookie is secure=True + path=/dashboard -> remote use needs TLS. 401 on bad password.

flows.name: Consumer logs into the dashboard with their API key (scoped view)
preconditions: valid active consumer token; DASHBOARD_SESSION_SECRET set

steps.expected: _caller_auth validates; mints consumer-role session {role:consumer,consumer,key_sha256}
Catalog/Provider-keys/Key-usage hidden; admin ops -> 403 dashboard_admin_required

flows.notes: Same endpoint as password login; api_key vs password selects the branch.

flows.name: Trusted-header SSO admin auth (behind a reverse proxy)
preconditions: both DASHBOARD_TRUSTED_USER_HEADER and DASHBOARD_TRUSTED_USER_SECRET set; a proxy that strips client copies

steps.action: proxy injects trusted user header + x-dashboard-trusted-secret
steps.expected: if secret matches via hmac.compare_digest -> admin session {user:trusted_user}

flows.notes: Disabled by default. Evaluated AFTER NO_AUTH, BEFORE the cookie. Secret header name is fixed; only the user header name is configurable.

flows.name: Log out of the dashboard

steps.expected: session cookie deleted; login card shown

flows.name: Consuming service authenticates to /v1 with its bearer token
flows.entry_point: Authorization: Bearer <per-service-token> on any /v1 path (point at ingress :8080, not router)
preconditions: stack running; valid active consumer token

steps.expected: standard OpenAI SDK usage
unknown/missing -> 401; inactive/revoked/expired -> 403

steps.action: ingress strips Authorization, adds x-llm-router-caller, proxies to router
steps.expected: router runs policy over the host's keys; caller never sends provider keys

flows.notes: Token maps to a caller name for audit. /x/* paths are 404'd for consumers. PUBLIC_BASE_URL shown in the key-handoff blurb.

flows.name: Configure the OpenRouter API key
flows.entry_point: env OPENROUTER_API_KEY in .env.secrets (provider openrouter/openrouter_market, auth_env=OPENROUTER_API_KEY); or dashboard provider-keys/update
preconditions: an OpenRouter account+key; .env.secrets mounted into router+ingress

steps.expected: host resolves Authorization: Bearer $OPENROUTER_API_KEY for openrouter calls
credits_usd balance + runway shown in dashboard

flows.notes: /models pricing discovery is keyless; the key is only needed to call and to read /credits. Tier=fallback.

flows.name: OpenRouter live whole-catalog discovery

steps.expected: long-tail exposed as marketplace offers; curated families skipped

steps.action: each model gets traits inline (benchmarks, modalities, cap_*, ranks)
steps.expected: discovered families rank on real benchmark not just price

flows.notes: Negative per-token prices (OpenRouter -1 sentinel) dropped so they can't win cost-led policies; $0 free models stay routable.

flows.name: Regenerate model_meta.lua (registered benchmark/modality/cap traits)

steps.expected: fetches /models; writes per-curated-family traits + ranks to model_meta.lua (GENERATED, do not hand-edit)

flows.notes: Only curated families land here (on-chain/deterministic path); discovered families carry traits inline. No money/credentials.

flows.name: Dashboard 'Add provider' (BYO OpenAI-compatible gateway)
preconditions: admin session; secrets/ mounted RW for ingress; .env.secrets writable

steps.action: POST {id, base_url, auth_env, tier, served_models[], key}
steps.expected: validate_entry (id regex, http(s) base_url, api_kind=openai_compatible only, UPPER_SNAKE auth_env, known families)
key -> .env.secrets under auth_env; provider def -> secrets/providers.local.json (key never stored there); /x/providers re-inits core preserving breaker/EMA; on failure loads next restart

flows.notes: Only openai_compatible providers can be added at runtime. Overlay never overwrites a config.live.lua provider. No per-tenant provider keys -- provider keys are global to the router.

flows.name: Update / reveal an existing provider credential
preconditions: admin session; provider has an auth_env (NOT the oauth/codex provider)

steps.expected: writes .env.secrets, hot-applies via /x/provider-key
raw env-key value + 12-char fingerprint; logged dashboard_provider_key_revealed

flows.notes: Update rejects providers with no auth_env (codex -> use the Codex flow). Reveal exposes the real key in plaintext to an admin session.

flows.name: Codex (ChatGPT-subscription) provider setup (codex login + mount auth.json)
flows.entry_point: codex login; env CODEX_AUTH_PATH (compose mounts host file -> /codex/auth.json); router --codex-auth; provider openai (api_kind=openai_codex, oauth)
preconditions: a paid ChatGPT subscription; codex CLI installed

steps.expected: writes ~/.codex/auth.json with access/refresh/id tokens + account_id
router picks it up lazily on first codex call
codex_backend POSTs chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses (SSE) with Bearer token + chatgpt-account-id

flows.notes: UNOFFICIAL / ToS-risky. access_token JWT expires in hours -> see codex-token-refresh; an expired token + dead refresh_token -> auth_error(401) -> provider disabled. Mount must be writable (token is rewritten on refresh). temperature/max_tokens NOT forwarded.

flows.name: Codex token auto-refresh (refresh_token -> access_token, written back)

steps.action: read JWT exp; if within 300s margin -> refresh
steps.expected: _needs_refresh() true
new tokens; _write_back to auth.json preserving layout

flows.notes: Refresh failures are swallowed (keep old token; 401 surfaces later). Thread-safe. If refresh_token is dead -> 401 -> provider disabled (the failure we hit). The mounted host file gets rewritten -> bind mount must be writable.

flows.name: Manage multiple Codex accounts from the dashboard
flows.entry_point: GET/POST /dashboard/api/codex/accounts ; DELETE /dashboard/api/codex/accounts/{name}; CodexAuthStore over CODEX_ACCOUNTS_DIR (default /codex/accounts on the PVC)
preconditions: admin session; auth.json blob(s) to paste

steps.expected: validated (must contain access_token), saved <slug>.json chmod 600, store reloaded, router /x/codex/reload
per-account {name,account_id,fingerprint} (never raw token) + active=sorted(names)[0] + activity (used_percent, recent_429, scarcity_price_in)
file unlinked, reloaded; 404 if missing

flows.notes: GOTCHA: active account = first alphabetically by name (per-call selection is a follow-up). Legacy single /codex/auth.json auto-adopted as account 'default' (which sorts before most names). In compose, /codex/accounts is NOT shared with the router by default -- the router reads only the mounted /codex/auth.json (a real wiring gap).

flows.name: Codex scarcity price ramp (ranking-only; bills $0)
flows.entry_point: sources/codex.py ingest()/poll; Config tab knobs (codex.quota_demote_start, imputed_price_in/out, quota_429_window_s, quota_429_shed)

steps.action: every codex call pushes an observation (status + quota headers) -- never probes
steps.expected: _demote_frac() = max(quota used fraction, recent-429 fraction)

steps.action: imputed ranking price = imputed_price_* x frac via update_metrics
steps.expected: paid routes take over before the 429 wall; price decays back as pressure ages out

flows.notes: Codex seeded at price ~0 in metrics.live.lua so cost-led policies rank it first; executed/billing cost stays $0. Ranking-only.

flows.name: Register a per-consumer ingress token via CLI (sha256 storage)

steps.expected: generates llmr_<token> (rejects <16 chars)
stores sha256(token)->consumer in CALLER_KEYS_SHA256_JSON; raw token printed once, never stored
legacy CALLER_KEYS_JSON (raw token, discouraged)

flows.notes: .env.secrets rewritten chmod 600. Restart/reload for keys to take effect (caller maps read at module import).

flows.name: Issue a consumer key from the dashboard (+ setup blurb)

steps.expected: mints <prefix>_<token>; sha256->consumer in CALLER_KEYS_SHA256_JSON; appends {sha256_prefix,status:active,created_at} record
raw api_key shown once; blurb has PUBLIC_BASE_URL, profile:default, curl + Python OpenAI-SDK examples, /usage, 403 route hint

flows.notes: Only hashed metadata persisted. The new key works immediately on the ingress (in-memory map updated). This is the path we used to mint the local test key.

flows.name: Rotate a consumer key with a grace period

steps.expected: new key minted

steps.action: existing active non-expiring keys get expires_at=now+grace (clamped 0..90d) + replaced_at
steps.expected: old keys work until expiry, then rejected caller_key_expired

flows.notes: Lets clients swap keys without downtime; expiry enforced at request time.

flows.name: Revoke a consumer key immediately

steps.expected: record status:revoked + revoked_at; hash dropped from CALLER_KEYS_SHA256_JSON (and plaintext); persisted to .env.secrets
404 if prefix not found; revoked key -> caller_key_revoked; takes effect immediately

flows.name: Configure consumer policy (status / allowed_routes / rate / burst)

steps.expected: inactive -> all that consumer's keys rejected (caller_inactive)
_route_allowed gates each request by model / profile:<name>; empty=all
per-caller 60s window; effective = max(rate_per_min, burst), default to global RATE_PER_MIN/BURST

flows.notes: Dashboard UI = Consumers tab -> Settings drawer (saveConsumerSettings).

flows.name: Reveal a recoverable consumer key (legacy plaintext only)

steps.expected: raw rows only for legacy CALLER_KEYS_JSON entries + hash_only_count
message: cannot be revealed; generate a replacement

flows.notes: Hash-only (default) keys are unrecoverable by design.

flows.name: Chat completion with the default policy (empty model)
preconditions: Bearer caller token (active, route allowed, under rate limit)

steps.expected: contract.profile=default_profile; the single declarative default Sigma_pol policy runs; max_tokens defaulted to 4096 if omitted

steps.action: router filters->scores->picks over the host keys
steps.expected: 200 chat.completion + x_router{provider, model_family, served_model_id, price_in/out, cost_usd, policy_fingerprint, decision_trace (ranked trimmed to top 10)}

flows.notes: Primary 'let the router decide' path. cost_usd = tokens x chosen price (0 for codex subs, clamped >=0).

flows.name: Chat completion pinned to a model family (model=family:NAME)

steps.expected: default policy + requirements.model_family; only that family qualifies
200; x_router.model_family = the pin; route key for allow-lists = 'family:deepseek-v3'

flows.notes: Sugar over the default policy.

flows.name: Chat completion pinned to one (provider, family) (model=pin:PROVIDER/FAMILY)

steps.expected: requirements.pin={provider,model}; only that exact seller qualifies; no cross-provider fallback
200 with x_router.provider==pin; if it errors -> exhausted/no_candidates (nothing to fall back to)

flows.notes: Malformed pin (no '/') silently degrades to plain default policy.

flows.name: Chat completion via a named profile (model=profile:NAME or path /NAME/v1/...)

steps.expected: contract.profile='edge'; for the path form the path wins and the model string is ignored
200; x_router.policy_fingerprint identifies the profile

flows.notes: Only 'default' ships out of the box. Route 'profile:edge' is derived from the path too, so route restriction works for both forms.

flows.name: Chat completion with a per-call Sigma_pol policy_ir term

steps.action: POST body with a policy_ir array term
steps.expected: shim forwards it verbatim; the CORE is the single admission boundary (check sorts/arity/depth<=64/nodes<=4096) then AND-composes the host policy_envelope (caller can only narrow)
200 with x_router.policy_fingerprint = term identity; admission failure -> 400 invalid_request_error code invalid_policy 'policy_ir rejected at admission: <reason>'

flows.notes: The PRIMARY routing path. 5-slot term: filter/score/pick/xform/failplan (core/docs/SIGMA-POL.md).

flows.name: Chat completion driving a Sigma_flow DAG (flow_ir term)

steps.action: POST body with flow_ir = ['flow', {input, llm nodes (each with own policy+system+inputs), output}]
steps.expected: flow_ir takes precedence over policy_ir/model; core admits the whole DAG (acyclic, one source/one sink, each node's policy admitted)

steps.action: execute_flow_async schedules the DAG; each llm node is a normal routed call
steps.expected: answer = sink output; x_router.provider='flow', model_family='flow:<fp>', decision_trace.flow_nodes[] per-node trace (provider/model/price/tokens/latency/nested trace)
admission -> 400 invalid_flow; a node failing surfaces the node's REAL error kind (e.g. no_candidates->503), not a blanket 502; failed flow still records provider:'flow' + failed_node

flows.notes: Sigma_flow = declarative composition over Sigma_pol (no loops/effects). Tokens ~ Nx input. This is the ensemble/review-MoA path we screenshotted.

flows.name: Streaming chat completion (stream:true, SSE)

steps.action: POST any routing form with stream:true
steps.expected: Content-Type text/event-stream; fallback BEFORE the first content delta is still a clean JSON error (not SSE)
role chunk, text deltas, final chunk with usage+x_router (cost+trimmed trace), then [DONE]; heartbeat comments keep the line warm under the 60s ALB idle timeout
ingress tees the SSE tail to extract final usage/cost -> real tokens in the usage row

flows.notes: A streaming Sigma_flow has no token stream -> first byte + heartbeats while it runs, then the assembled result (or trace+error on failure).

flows.name: Chat completion with tool calls

steps.action: POST with tools[]/tool_choice (+ any routing form)
steps.expected: shim forwards tools/tool_choice/response_format/temperature/seed; a policy can gate on cap_tools
200 with choices[0].message.tool_calls, finish_reason 'tool_calls'; in a flow the sink node's tool_calls are emitted

flows.notes: flow+tools with no tool-capable candidate fails clean with no_candidates (503), not 502.

flows.name: List the routable model catalog

steps.expected: {object:list, data:[{id:'profile:<name>'},{id:'family:<fam>'}...]} = profile names + curated + discovered families; exactly the model-field values a caller can send

flows.notes: Auth required (goes through the proxy).

flows.name: Per-key usage self-service (consumer)
preconditions: Bearer caller token (the exact key whose usage is reported)

steps.expected: usage for ONLY that key; window 15m/24h/7d/4w overrides since; limit clamped 1-500
schema_version 3, key_sha256_prefix (first 12 hex only), consumer_settings, totals, cost_estimate, daily/monthly, by_provider/model_family/route/served_model/status, route_health, recent[]

flows.notes: Bad window -> 400 invalid_usage_window. Operator equivalent: POST /dashboard/api/key-usage (admin session).

flows.name: Auth / route-restriction / rate-limit rejections (ingress)

steps.expected: 401 caller_auth
403 caller_inactive | caller_key_revoked | caller_key_expired

steps.action: route not in allowed_routes (route = body.model or profile:<seg> from path)
steps.expected: 403 caller_route_not_allowed; empty/['all']/['*'] = all; 'prefix*' wildcard
429 caller_rate_limit

flows.notes: All enforced in the ingress BEFORE forwarding; rejects recorded (_record_reject).

flows.name: Fallback/cascade behavior + router error kinds (in the trace)

steps.expected: router tries ranked candidates in order; decision_path records each attempt (provider/family, error_kind, http_status, message). 402->payment_required, 401/403->auth_error, 429->rate_limit, 408/504->timeout, 404->model_unavailable, 400->bad_request/context_overflow, 5xx->server_error, empty content->bad_response

error body {error{message,type:router_error,code}, x_router{decision_trace}}; mapped: no_candidates->503, auth_error->401, rate_limit->429, bad_request/context_overflow->400, timeout->504, else->502; message appends per-attempt summary

flows.notes: This is exactly what we debugged (antseed payment_required/network_error, z-ai/glm-5.2 bad_response, opus bad_request). Same translation on the streaming path.

flows.name: View Analytics (spend / traffic / errors with filters)

steps.expected: metric cards Requests/Spend/Tokens/Success-rate; over-time bars; By provider/model/consumer/status tables; live chat-health banner

steps.action: change timeframe (all/runtime/1h/24h/7d/30d), consumer, provider, model filters; Refresh
steps.expected: snapshot rescoped/refetched; 15s auto-poll

flows.name: Activity -- per-request trace (policy term, fallback chain, cost, Sigma_flow DAG)
flows.entry_point: Activity tab -> rows from /dashboard/api/stats recent[]; filter All/Requests/Rejects/Probes

steps.expected: table Time/Event/Caller/Status/Route/Provider/Cost/Error
meta pills (policy/flow fingerprint, cost, tokens, latency, served model); Attempts fallback order with per-provider ok/error/skip; copyable Sigma_pol term
node DAG view (level-laid nodes, per-node provider/model/latency/tokens + inner attempts)

flows.notes: This is the view we used for the flow screenshots.

flows.name: Builder -- compose / review / download / test a Sigma_pol policy

steps.action: compose filter rows / score terms / selector (rows<->raw term)
steps.expected: bBuildTerm assembles ['policy',filter,score,selector,...]; fields data-driven from /x/fields
normalize -> fingerprint; preview (/x/rank) -> ranked Provider/Model/Tier/$in/$out/Score; empty survivors -> 'No survivors'
sigma-pol-<fp>.json {version,fingerprint,policy_ir}
runs /v1/chat/completions with policy_ir; result chips + output; recorded in Activity (caller=dashboard-test, not billed)

flows.notes: 400s render verbatim in the builder error box.

flows.name: Flow Builder -- compose / review / download / test a Sigma_flow DAG

steps.action: add nodes, per-node system prompt, choose inputs (multi-input=fusion), pick answer node; '↧ use Policy-builder term' or edit raw
steps.expected: fBuildIR assembles ['flow',{u:input,n*:llm,out:output}]
/dashboard/api/flow/normalize -> fingerprint, 'Flow admitted'
sigma-flow-<fp>.json
/dashboard/api/flow/test runs live; per-node trace chips + output; recorded in Activity

flows.notes: Default example = Mixture-of-agents (2 drafts -> synthesize), same shape as our ensemble.

flows.name: Catalog (Market) -- price book + per-seller perf, + SKILL.md download

steps.expected: per-family rows: quality, benchmark badges, modalities, cheapest $in/$out, seller count
seller table: wire model, $in/$out, status (live/disabled/pinned/over-cap), perf (success%/ms/calls), refreshed time
filtered list; catalog JSON to clipboard; SKILL.md with live catalog + field vocab baked in

flows.notes: Wallet cell appears on the AntSeed provider. 502 if router /x/market unavailable.

flows.name: Consumers tab -- browse / scope / settings / create-rotate-revoke-reveal keys

steps.expected: rows (status, requests/errors/tokens/spend/last-seen/key-status); scope to one consumer -> detail + routes/providers breakdown + recent activity
Save settings (configure-consumer-limits); Generate/rotate key (+ handoff blurb); Revoke key; Reveal keys -- see flows 25-29

flows.notes: Consumer-role sessions get admin actions hidden.

flows.name: Key usage -- operator per-key lookup
flows.entry_point: Key usage tab -> POST /dashboard/api/key-usage {api_key, window?, limit?, offset?}

steps.expected: same sanitized router_key_usage snapshot as /v1/usage for that key; 404 key_usage_not_found if unseen; 401 if not admin

flows.notes: Consumer bearer does NOT satisfy this -- admin session only.

flows.name: Provider keys tab -- view / reveal / edit / add / manage Codex accounts

steps.expected: rows: provider, credential (env name+fingerprint) or AntSeed wallet cell, usage stats; reveal -> raw key inline

steps.action: edit / add provider / manage codex accounts
steps.expected: see flows provider-add, provider-key-update-reveal, codex-multi-account

flows.notes: Privatized by default (env names + fingerprints).

flows.name: Config tab -- per-provider runtime knobs (PVC-backed, hot-apply)

steps.expected: knobs grouped by provider (antseed/codex/openrouter): value, default, [min,max], override pill
validated vs schema, persisted to PVC, hot-applied; 'Saved · live'; 400 on schema failure
override cleared to default

flows.notes: Knobs: antseed top-N/runway, codex scarcity ramp/runway, openrouter credit runway.

flows.name: AntSeed buyer identity + fund the hot-wallet (USDC + ETH gas on Base mainnet)
flows.entry_point: env ANTSEED_IDENTITY_HEX (private key) ; on-chain transfer to the wallet address
preconditions: a secp256k1 private key controlling a Base wallet; real USDC + ETH

steps.action: set ANTSEED_IDENTITY_HEX to the funded wallet's private key
steps.expected: durable identity across volume loss (unset -> ephemeral key in the volume, lost with it)

steps.action: send USDC + ETH(gas) to the wallet address
steps.expected: raw wallet USDC appears (but is NOT yet spendable -- see escrow)

flows.notes: REAL MONEY. Treat ANTSEED_IDENTITY_HEX as a private key -- never commit it. wallet USDC alone does nothing; the buyer spends from escrow.

flows.name: AntSeed deposit / withdraw / refresh (wallet <-> escrow)
flows.entry_point: dashboard Deposit/Withdraw/Refresh -> POST /dashboard/api/wallet/{deposit,withdraw,refresh} -> /x/wallet/* -> sidecar control :8379 -> antseed buyer <cmd>
preconditions: funded wallet; ANTSEED_CONTROL_TOKEN set on router+sidecar (else 503); in k8s ANTSEED_CONTROL_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8379

steps.expected: amount validated ^\d+(\.\d{1,6})?$ >0; antseed buyer deposit <amt> on-chain (120s tx timeout); mutations serialized
status-antseed.json rewritten; router reads new depositsAvailable on next poll
new escrow + runway (off available+reserved total; reserved returns as channels settle)

flows.notes: REAL on-chain spend. wallet-vs-escrow gotcha: dashboard shows depositsAvailable (escrow), what the buyer spends -- NOT raw wallet USDC. Prod runbook = the antseed-prod-deposit skill (VPN+SSO+kubectl). Propagation lag: sidecar 60s, router poll 300s.

flows.name: AntSeed browse market / regenerate price seeds (no spend)
flows.entry_point: antseed network browse --services --json --top 50 ; auto /market/market.json ; dashboard Market via /x/market

steps.expected: per-peer servicePricing[<model>] input/output USD-per-million, queryable WITHOUT spending

steps.action: seed metrics.live.lua with the cheapest peer per model
steps.expected: a seed only; live EMA becomes source of truth once proxies run

flows.notes: Stale dump (>900s) -> degraded (no antseed candidates). Writer validates JSON before writing. Caps enforced by the buyer proxy so a stale seed can't overpay.
